one_liner: Ordered, deduplicated pub/sub messaging that fans out to multiple SQS FIFO queues or other subscribers with strict message ordering.
when_to_use: Need ordered, exactly-once message fanout to multiple consumers
Event-driven architectures requiring strict ordering guarantees across multiple subscribers
Financial transactions, inventory updates, or other workflows where message order matters
Replacing point-to-point ordered queues with ordered pub/sub patterns
when_not_to_use: High-throughput scenarios exceeding 300 messages/second per message group (use standard SNS + application-level ordering)
Subscribers that do not need ordering guarantees (use standard SNS topics for higher throughput)
Streaming data ingestion (use Kinesis Data Streams)
key_facts: SNS FIFO topics deliver messages in strict order within a message group, with exactly-once processing via deduplication.
Can fan out directly to multiple SQS FIFO queue subscriptions. You do NOT need a single SQS queue to receive and re-distribute.
Supports up to 300 messages/second (or 10 MB/second) per FIFO topic, with up to 3,000 messages/second with batching.
Message groups allow parallel processing of independent message streams while maintaining order within each group.
Content-based deduplication or explicit deduplication IDs prevent duplicate delivery.
Currently supports SQS FIFO queues as subscribers. Does not support Lambda, HTTP, or email subscriptions directly (route through SQS FIFO to Lambda).
Topic name must end with .fifo suffix.
pricing_summary: SNS FIFO: $0.50 per million published messages + $0.06 per million subscription deliveries. SQS FIFO subscribers: standard SQS FIFO pricing applies.
common_misconceptions: SNS FIFO CAN fan out to multiple subscribers directly. You do NOT need a single SQS FIFO queue with Lambda to re-distribute messages to other queues.
SNS FIFO ordering is per message group, NOT per topic. Different message groups can be processed in parallel.
SNS FIFO does NOT support all subscription protocols. Currently limited to SQS FIFO queues as subscribers.
FIFO topics and Standard topics are different resources. You cannot convert between them after creation.
